revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:浜降祭]]
'''Hamaori Festival''' (浜降祭) is a {{ill|festival|ja|祭り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祭|1=Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q132241}} held annually at {{ill|Nishihama Beach|ja|西浜海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nishihama Beach|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, on {{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|qid=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}} in July. The festival features processions and parades of portable shrines carried with chants of {{ill|Jinku|en|Jinku|qid=Q11574321|lt=Jinku|1=Jinku}} and a unique "Dokkoikatsugi" carrying style.
== Overview == The Hamaori Festival involves parishioners of various {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Samukawa|en|Samukawa|qid=Q1342787|lt=Samukawa|1=Samukawa}} and Chigasaki carrying their {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} into the sea, performing purification rites, and then returning to their communities while parading the mikoshi. In the early Heisei era, the festival was rougher, with mikoshi sometimes being dunked into the sea, but recent practices focus on gentler forms to reduce the costs of mikoshi repair. Consequently, more women and children now participate.
In recent years (since the 21st century), mikoshi from distant shrines are often transported by truck to Nishihama Beach, while participants travel by chartered buses, mostly provided by {{ill|Kanagawa Chuo Transportation|ja|神奈川中央交通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanagawa Chuo Transportation|1=Kanagawa Chuo Kotsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10854960}}. Previously, mikoshi were carried on foot from their shrines to the beach, starting from midnight and arriving at dawn.
== History == === Origins === The origins of the Hamaori Festival are attributed to three theories, with the third being the most credible: 1. In {{ill|1838|en|1838|qid=Q7612|lt=1838|1=1838}} or {{ill|1840|en|1840|qid=Q7619|lt=1840|1=1840}}, after a dispute between parishioners of {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|qid=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine|1=Samukawa Shrine}} and {{ill|Maedori Shrine|ja|前鳥神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maedori Shrine|1=Sakitori Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11397865}} over a mikoshi during the return from the {{ill|Kōnomachi Festival|ja|国府祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnomachi Festival|1=Kokufu-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11420919}}, the mikoshi was lost in the {{ill|Sagami River|en|Sagami River|qid=Q612253|lt=Sagamigawa River|1=Sagami River}} and later found by a local fisherman, Suzuki Magosaburō, initiating purification rituals at Nanko Beach. 2. According to the ''{{ill|Shinpen Sagami Koku Fudoki Kō|ja|新編相模国風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Sagami Koku Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Sagami no Kuni Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503942}}'', Sasazuka Daimyōjin (later merged with {{ill|Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine|ja|鶴嶺八幡宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurumine Hachiman Shrine|qq=}}) performed purification rites at the beach long before Samukawa Shrine's participation. 3. Prior to the mikoshi loss incident, both Samukawa Shrine and Tsurumine Hachiman independently performed purification rites, which were later unified at Nanko Beach.
=== Timeline === * 1030: {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|en|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qid=Q3082851|lt=Minamoto no Yorinobu|1=Minamoto no Yoriyoshi}} prays for victory at Tsurumine Hachiman. * 1838/1840: The mikoshi loss incident occurs. * 1876: The festival date is shifted to {{ill|July 15|en|July 15|qid=Q2686|lt=July 15|1=July 15}}, renamed as the "Hamaori Festival." * 1997: The date is moved to {{ill|Marine Day|en|Marine Day|qid=Q1355833|lt=Marine Day|1=Marine Day}}, initially observed on July 20. * 2004: The date adjusts to the third Monday of July following a revision of the {{ill|Holiday Law|ja|祝日法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Holiday Law|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国民の祝日に関する法律|1=Act on National Holidays|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11421011}}.
== Participating Shrines == As of the Heisei era, approximately 30 shrines participate annually. Below is the 2003 list:
=== Samukawa Town === * {{ill|Samukawa Shrine|en|Samukawa Shrine|qid=Q48744|lt=Samukawa Shrine|1=Samukawa Shrine}} * {{ill|Sugaya Shrine|ja|菅谷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugaya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504624|1=Sugaya Shrine}} * {{ill|Kurami Shrine|ja|倉見神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurami Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504626|1=Kurami Shrine}} * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Daijin Shrine|ja|八幡大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Daijin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八幡神}}
=== Chigasaki City === * {{ill|Chayamachi Daijingū|ja|茶屋町大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi Daijingū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504627|1=Chayamachi Daijingū}} * {{ill|Yakumo Shrine|ja|八雲神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakumo Shrine|1=Yakumo shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11391847}} * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Konpira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} ...
== Related Festivals == * {{ill|Ōoka Echizen Festival|ja|大岡越前祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Echizen Festival|1=Ōoka Echizen Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434756}} * {{ill|Shonan Festival|ja|湘南祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shonan Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
== Related Works == * ''{{ill|Chigasaki Story: My Little Hometown|ja|茅ヶ崎物語 〜MY LITTLE HOMETOWN〜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chigasaki Story: My Little Hometown|1=Chigasaki Monogatari: 〜MY LITTLE HOMETOWN〜|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q109598151}}'' (2017 documentary featuring the festival) * {{ill|Kaze no Uta o Kikasete|ja|風の詩を聴かせて|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaze no Uta o Kikasete|1=Kaze no Uta o Kikasete|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665377}} (song by {{ill|Keisuke Kuwata|en|Keisuke Kuwata|qid=Q1197175|lt=Keisuke Kuwata|1=Keisuke Kuwata}})
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.samukawa-jinja.or.jp/movie/hama.htm Video of Hamaori Festival at Samukawa Shrine (archived)]
